title: Distinguish Missing Values from Invalid Computed Values
summary: Build and verify css custom property fallbacks in field manual token failure and fallback experiment through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css custom property fallbacks from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css custom property fallbacks without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 03 · Read the field manual token failure and fallback experiment case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim preserves css custom property fallbacks when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: field manual token failure and fallback experiment.
steps.why: Distinguish Missing Values from Invalid Computed Values makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in field manual token failure and fallback experiment.

content.text: The second argument to var() is used when the referenced custom property is missing or guaranteed-invalid; it can contain commas and another var(). A present value can still make the consuming declaration invalid at computed-value time, causing that property to fall back according to CSS rules rather than automatically selecting the var fallback. Fallback chains should be short, intentional, and tested by removing and corrupting each value. Apply it to field manual token failure and fallback experiment.
